title: France And The Limits Of Eurozone Spread Compression
text:
France And The Limits Of Eurozone Spread Compression

Cedric Gemehl

Gavekal Research | 10 Jul 2026

With 21 members, there is always an election around the corner in the eurozone. The 2027 electoral calendar, however, is unusually heavy. France holds the first round of its presidential election on April 18, followed by the runoff on May 2, while Spain and Italy must hold general elections by August 22 and December 22, respectively. Together, these three countries account for 59% of the eurozone’s €13.9trn public debt stock. This raises a simple question: can eurozone sovereign spreads compress much further as political risk mounts next year?The short answer is that the scope for further compression is limited. The eurozone’s sovereign bond market structure and fiscal risk profile have changed profoundly since the 2012 crisis. Investors no longer need to price in a breakup of the currency union, but France’s persistent political dysfunction remains a key constraint on further spread compression.
